question.text: Which was the first known organized futures market in the world?

answer.text: Osaka Rice Exchange, set up in 1730
Chicago Board of Trade, set up in 1848
London Metal Exchange, set up in 1877
Bombay Cotton Trade Association, set up in 1875

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The Osaka Rice Exchange in Japan, established in 1730, is recognized as the first organized futures market. Rice bills issued by sellers evolved into futures-like instruments. **The Trap:** CBOT (1848) and LME (1877) are more widely known but came later. Bombay Cotton Trade Association (1875) was the first in India, not globally. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII (Equity Derivatives) and Series I (Currency Derivatives) also cover the evolution of derivatives markets. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Understanding the historical evolution helps explain why commodity futures have unique features like physical delivery — they originated from actual agricultural trade needs.
